arts.ttl: Manchester City climb into Premier League top four and Forest slip up at home
'No-one can do what Kevin has' - De Bruyne shines as Man City exit nears
arts.sum: Kevin de Bruyne has three more Manchester City games to play at Etihad Stadium - and three more opportunities for City fans to give him a standing ovation.

arts.ttl: Barcelona youngster could be tempted into transfer amid Bayern & Man City interest
arts.sum: One of the most highly-regarded young members of the academy setup at La Liga giants Barcelona is giving serious consideration to a summer departure from the club. That’s according to…

arts.ttl: Jean-Philippe Mateta: Oliver Glasner hits out at Crystal Palace striker as he explains half-time substitution in Man City defeat
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner explains why he decided to take off top scorer Jean-Philippe Mateta at half time in Crystal Palace's 5-2 defeat to Manchester City at the Etihad Stadium.

arts.ttl: Pep hails departing KDB after rout: 'Incredible'
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola has admitted that no player can replicate the "incredible" Kevin De Bruyne after the departing Manchester City midfielder starred in the 5-2 win over Crystal Palace on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace Must Do Everything to Keep ‘Incredible’ Man Amid Exit Talks
arts.sum: Amidst discussions around the future of a number of their players, Crystal Palace must ensure that they keep hold of their in-demand manager.

arts.ttl: We'll all miss Kevin De Bruyne's genius - and Pep Guardiola will miss him more than most
arts.sum: MANCHESTER CITY 5-2 CRYSTAL PALACE: Kevin De Bruyne turned the tide for City as they battled back from two goals down to earn a crucial win in their challenge for the top five

arts.ttl: Palace boss: City loss had nothing to do with missing suspended captain Guehi
arts.sum: Palace took a two-goal lead in the early stages of the match thanks to goals from Eberechi Eze and Chris Richards, but silly mistakes and a significant drop-off in performance levels saw the lead slip away before half-time. “Every team misses his captain, but it was more that Chris Richards did not train for 10 days, Maxence Lacroix did not train the whole week, so everybody was not on his top level.

arts.ttl: Crystal Palace boss critical of defensive showing in 5-2 loss at Manchester City
arts.sum: Oliver Glasner said his defenders were not good enough as Crystal Palace saw a 2-0 lead crumble into a 5-2 defeat at the Etihad today. Omar Marmoush pulled the hosts level before half-time, with goals from Mateo Kovacic, James McAtee and Nico O’Reilly bringing the final score to a thumping 5-2 defeat for Crystal Palace.

arts.ttl: Kevin De Bruyne determined to leave Champions League legacy for Man City
arts.sum: The departing playmaker inspired a comeback win over Crystal Palace which could be important in the race for the top five.
